---
title: "Pipeline"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

The measurement is a [Kedro](https://kedro.org) project: pure algorithms in `transfer_risk.lib`, thin nodes that read and write through a typed Data Catalog, and pipelines wired one stage at a time (data, models, similarity, attacks, transfer, risk, reporting). The graph below is the live Kedro-Viz export — pan, zoom, and click a node or dataset to see its inputs, outputs, and layer.

This is the full production graph: ten surrogates, five attack recipes, and the attack stage's one-node-per-`(surrogate, recipe, example-shard)` fan-out — about 2,450 attack-shard nodes, and roughly 5,000 nodes once datasets are counted — which `ParallelRunner` runs across the box's cores and `kedro run --only-missing-outputs` resumes. It is a large graph and takes a moment to lay out; use the controls to zoom, focus a single pipeline or node, and collapse the view.

```{=html}
<iframe src="pipeline-viz/index.html" title="Kedro pipeline graph"
        style="width:100%; height:82vh; border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #dee2e6); border-radius:4px;">
</iframe>
```
